Romance is a paradigm on [[sexuality]] inspired by [[romanticism]].

It values the idea of gaining transcendence through emotions over rationality as with other forms of romanticism. In its case it is especially about sexuality, while other strains like [[romantic nationalism]] focus on different areas<ref>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Noc1OH0CUBc&list=PLND1JCRq8Vuh3f0P5qjrSdb5eC1ZfZwWJ&index=25</ref>.

Romance is associated with [[marriage]].

[[Eusexuality]] is an alternative position to romance, and it is associated with [[syzygy]] rather than marriage.

== References ==
[[Category:Sexuality]]
[[sn:Romance]][[Category:Hydroplanes]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
[[sn:Romance]][[sn:Romance]][[Category:Romance]]
[[sn:Romance]][[sn:Romance]][[sn:Romance]][[Category:Romanticism]]
[[Category:Git synced pages']]
[[sn:Romance]][[Category:John Vervaeke]]
[[sn:Romance]]
[[Category:Pages]]
